page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 80 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, providing two threads per core. The system is configured with 80.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1.0 GB per core ratio. Cache specifications include 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the n2d-highcpu-80 demonstrates strong performance, particularly in multi-core and parallel processing tasks. Its stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 107952.86 and single-core score of 2693.85 both rank in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, reaching 5077.68 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 187.06 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The server also excels in cryptographic operations, software compilation with Clang, and ray tracing, all placing in the top 25% tier. Conversely, single-threaded decompression and LLM inference tasks fall into the average performance tier.

This server offers a specialized hardware profile that maximizes compute density relative to memory capacity. By omitting bundled local storage and GPUs, it provides a cost-efficient option for workloads that are heavily CPU-bound but have modest memory requirements. The high vCPU count and strong multi-core performance make it well-suited for parallel processing tasks, software compilation, cryptographic processing, and database operations such as Redis, while it is less optimal for memory-intensive databases or GPU-accelerated machine learning.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-80 is a compute-focused x86_64 server featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs and 80.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 40 physical cores. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this server is designed to maximize CPU density. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent cryptographic capabilities, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems. Conversely, single-threaded tasks and LLM inference speeds align with average tier performance. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not demand large memory footprints. It is highly suited for software compilation, ray tracing, cryptographic processing, and high-throughput database operations like Redis.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 server featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs, 80 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-80 features 80 dedicated vCPUs, 80 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for high-density compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core compute server optimized for parallel processing, cryptography, and software compilation without the overhead of excess memory.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated vCPUs and 40 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7B13
80.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Strong multi-core CPU, cryptographic, and software compilation performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators to minimize resource overhead
Best suited for parallel processing, ray tracing, and Redis database operations
